violations.text: A drip was observed at the beverage line at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

The floor tiles around the pipe near the public restroom are in poor repair. Inspector observed missing tiles and holes in the floor. PIC contacted maintenance for repair during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2025

An employee was observed not washing their hands after returning from outside and before returning to work. PIC instructed staff to wash their hands. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
The hand-washing sink in the basement prep area was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. ice machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. PIC called for service. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025

Several foods were observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods requiring refrigeration were disposed. Other foods moved to alternative refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025

Working containers of sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
A deceased cockroach was observed on glue pads in basement prep area. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
Bowls of herbs (prep unit) and a bowl of potato (counter) were observed in direct contact with the food below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025
A knife was observed stored between prep units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
Four wire baskets were observed with chipped coating or frayed wires. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
The following were observed with dust and debris: -ice machine vents -kitchen exhaust fan -blender bases -bulk food storage containers. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2025
The hand-washing sink at the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

Vacuum packaged tilapia was observed thawing in prep unit at 34 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC informed that vaccuum packaged seafood must be removed from reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing. Packages were opened during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024
Door gaskets for several prep unit low boy doors were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024
The floors in the bar area and kitchen is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2024
The hand-washing sink at the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
Flour in cambro container was observed adulterated. Lid was gnawed leaving material residue in food. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024
Various types of rodenticide were observed throughout the establishment. PIC informed to cease practice and remove from the premises. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cambros (5 gallon containers) of Sancocho were placed hot in the refrigerator. Foods were placed into smaller containers and over icebaths to continue cooling process. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

Vacuum packaged tilapia was observed thawing in the walk-in cooler at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC informed that vacuum packaged seafood must be removed from reduced oxygen environment prior to thawing. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

The doors to the outside (outside and kitchen backdoor), are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The presence of large rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings, gnawed container of food, and impacted physical facilities. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Ice used for drinks was observed in direct contact with soda lines. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Door gaskets for several prep unit low boy doors were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The outside dumpsters were not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Trash and litter was also observed on the ground surrounding refuse receptacles. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The following has an accumulation of soil residue: 1. Floors in basement office (apparent rodent droppings) 2. Wall tiles in prep room (behind ice machine) 3. Storage space under exterior stairs 4. Floors at bar especially under equipment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1. Floors in kitchen (COB: 04/26/24) 2. Door Archway in basement prep room (COB: 03/26/24) 3. Sewage drain lid, opening observed (03/11/24) 4. Ceiling near walk-in cooler and dry storage shelves (opening observed, gnawed insulation and apparent rodent droppings) (COB: 03/11/24) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement prep room and dry storage areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
